task_instructions: tesa TDS files often present concise property tables and application notes with fewer marketing sections than 3M.
Watch for tesa product codes that appear as 4 to 6 digit families with suffix or thickness variants.
brand_or_domain_conventions: tesa layouts often put product structure and key values near the first property table.
tesa property labels may be short and direct, so prefer dictionary matching over free interpretation.
table_patterns: tesa physical property tables are often compact and should be parsed row by row.
Application or handling sections may appear before full performance detail tables.
negative_rules: Do not over-split short tesa notes into many fake parameters.
Do not infer hidden variants unless the code is clearly shown.
few_shot_examples: A liner row usually maps to liner configuration, not a free-text unmatched note.
A short tesa property table should become one item plus several main fields and parameter values.
